entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: A region in Africa characterized by lower agricultural production compared to the rest of the world

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of producing crops and livestock for food and other products

entities.name: CLIMATE
entities.description: Environmental factors often cited as a reason for low agricultural production in Africa

entities.name: SOIL QUALITY
entities.description: The physical and chemical properties of soil that affect crop growth and productivity

entities.name: SLAVERY
entities.description: A historical system of forced labor cited as a factor affecting African development

entities.name: DISEASE
entities.description: Pathological conditions affecting humans or crops, cited as a factor for low production

entities.name: EUROPEAN TRADERS
entities.description: People from Europe who interacted with and manipulated African agricultural systems

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS
entities.description: Organized methods of farming and food production that existed in Africa before external interference

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: MANUFACTURING
entities.description: The process of making products from raw materials, supported by early African agricultural systems

entities.name: TRADE
entities.description: The exchange of goods and services, supported by early African agricultural systems

entities.name: EXPORT CROPS
entities.description: Crops grown specifically for sale to other countries, which led to the manipulation of local agricultural systems

entities.name: INDEPENDENCE
entities.description: The political freedom of African nations from external colonial control

entities.name: RESOURCE AND WEALTH EXTRACTION
entities.description: The removal of valuable materials and money from Africa for external benefit

entities.name: ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of improving the economic, political, and social well-being of a people

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The continent where the discussed agricultural and historical trends are occurring
